extraction.one_line_summary: MATTHEWS Michael attacked with ~500m to go on the uphill finish into Rodez, dropping VAN AVERMAET and winning solo with a no-hands celebration.
tactical_patterns: Sunweb collaborated with BMC from race start to control dangerous breakaway, sharing workload despite both teams having stage ambitions.
Froome's Team Sky exploited Aru's positioning error in valley before Rodez climb, accelerating when Astana leader isolated without teammates.
Matthews launched final attack at 500m on uphill finish after following Van Avermaet's wheel through technical corners, unusually early sprint.
Kittel dropped on moderate climbing approach to Rodez with three teammates, exposing pure sprinter vulnerability on rolling stage finishes.

rider_signals.ARU Fabio: negative: Caught at back of peloton without teammates in valley before final climb, losing 24 seconds and yellow jersey through poor positioning awareness
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: positive: Won after doing extensive leadout work for green jersey battle against Kittel earlier in Tour, revealing exceptional condition depth
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: positive: Team Sky exploited Aru's isolation with coordinated acceleration despite not having numerical advantage, reclaiming yellow through tactical opportunism
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: neutral: Moves to strong GC position (mentioned as 'sitting pretty' going into Alps), potentially complicating Sky's team hierarchy with Froome back in yellow
rider_signals.VAN AVERMAET Greg: neutral: Covered multiple attacks in finale but unable to respond to Matthews' 500m surge on uphill finish
